pulse: The S&P 500 gained 0.61% to close at 7,519.12, while the Nasdaq Composite jumped 1.19% to 26,656.18.    The S&P 500 gained 0.7% on Tuesday and the Nasdaq climbed more than 1% to fresh record highs while the Dow Jones reversed earlier gains and was down about 80 points as investors returned from the long weekend with continued focus on developments in the Middle East.

Steps have been taken to streamline operations leading to improved financials, with a 32% Y/Y increase in core pre-provision earnings and $6.3B in share repurchases expected to further boost earnings. why_paras:
What Happened:  
Shares of Micron Technology jumped 19% and topped $1 trillion in market capitalization amid bullishness among analysts on the Street.

Why It Happened:  
Wall Street kicked off the holiday-shortened week with stocks joining bonds higher as hopes for a peace deal between the US and Iran overshadowed military strikes in the Persian Gulf. Expectations of an agreement drove the S&P 500 toward a record, with chipmakers leading the charge. Treasury yields fell as concerns over a flare-up in inflation eased, making traders pare back their wagers on near-term Federal Reserve rate hikes.
U.S. consumer confidence moved lower in May, a survey from The Conference Board showed Tuesday, citing escalating inflationary impacts due to the Middle East conflict. The consumer confidence index dropped 0.7 to 93.1 from an upwardly revised 93.8 in April.

What It Means for Investors:  The divergence between the tech-led rally and broader market weakness—particularly in the Dow—signals concentration risk in artificial intelligence and semiconductor stocks. 
Two-thirds of consumers cited cutting back on spending overall due to rising prices, as of May.
 This consumer pullback contrasts sharply with the market's optimism on geopolitical resolution, creating volatility risk if Iran peace talks stall or if earnings disappoint amid soft consumer demand.
macro: Iran ceasefire hopes are easing oil prices and Treasury yields, but geopolitical uncertainty remains high and further military escalation could reverse gains quickly.
Consumer confidence edged downward in May as the inflationary impacts of the war in the Middle East intensified,

econ.why_it_matters: Critical inflation gauge excluding food/energy that influences Fed policy decisions on interest rates.
Measures economic growth rate; strong growth supports equity valuations especially for tech.
Labor market health indicator affecting Fed policy outlook and consumer spending dynamics.

stw.name: Micron Technology
stw.reason:
MU — Micron Technology  | Price: $902.88 |  BUY  | Horizon: 5-YEAR

Strong momentum with 20%+ daily surges and $1T market cap milestone signal market validation of AI memory demand thesis.

stw.name: Tesla
stw.reason:
TSLA — Tesla  | Price: $432.33 |  SELL  | Horizon: Exit Now
